scenario_name: Scenario 15 — Concurrency Bug in Async JavaScript
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 500 simultaneous DB/service calls and connection pool exhaustion clearly identified as the primary risk, (2) concurrency limiting recommended with p-limit example and specific concurrency value, (3) does NOT invent a false shared-state race condition — correctly avoids claiming JS integer increments are unsafe, (4) TypeScript err.message type safety noted as a critical finding with correct fix, (5) safe_to_deploy is false. Additionally provides valuable extras: timeout/cancellation concern, Promise.allSettled suggestion, and actionable fixes throughout.

observations.text: Correctly identifies unbounded concurrency (500 simultaneous requests) as the core problem with concrete reproduction steps
Provides specific p-limit fix with code example and sensible default concurrency of 20
Does not fabricate a JavaScript race condition on shared counters — avoids the common false positive trap
err.message type safety identified with correct instanceof Error guard pattern
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rationale
Bonus: identifies the missing timeout/cancellation risk and Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled semantics — both real concerns
High confidence level (95) is well-calibrated given the clear-cut nature of the findings
